title: Multiple Choice
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.question: What is the author's main argument in the opening section of the article?

options.B: AI companies should ignore government regulations.
options.C: Control over frontier AI has become a major source of American power.
options.D: Anthropic is the world's only successful AI company.

questions.question: Why did the Trump administration order Anthropic to restrict access to Fable and Mythos?

options.B: Because of concerns over a possible jailbreak.
options.C: To prepare the company for its IPO.

questions.question: According to the article, why is AI compared with nuclear technology and cryptography?

options.A: All three technologies were developed by the US military.
options.B: They all became obsolete over time.
options.C: Each raises questions about controlling access to powerful technology.
options.D: Each technology was freely shared with allies.

questions.question: What challenge do open-weight AI models present?

options.B: They may allow advanced capabilities to spread widely.
options.C: They require government approval before downloading.
options.D: They are less useful than previous AI systems.

questions.question: Why would restricting AI research too heavily be harmful to America?

options.A: Most AI research is funded by Europe.
options.B: American companies rely on international talent and customers.
options.C: China already controls the AI market.

questions.question: What does the author predict about future access to frontier AI?

options.A: Every country will receive the same AI models.
options.B: Only military organisations will use AI.
options.C: Access will likely depend on strategic importance.
options.D: Open-source AI will replace commercial AI completely.

questions.question: According to the article, what gives America another major advantage besides leading AI models?
Which recommendation does the author make for Europe and East Asia?

options.B: Depend more heavily on American AI companies.
options.D: Expand computing capacity and build stronger domestic AI ecosystems.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'vassal status' suggest in the article?

options.A: Europe could become heavily dependent on American AI.
options.D: Space-based data centres will replace all existing infrastructure.

questions.question: What is the author's overall conclusion?

options.B: The best response is to build technological strength rather than complain.
options.C: Open-weight AI should become the global standard immediately.
options.D: International cooperation on AI is impossible.
